title: Divine Epigrams: On the Miracle of the Multiplied Loaves by Richard Crashaw
content:
See here an easy feast that knows no wound,
That under hunger's teeth will needs be sound;
A subtle harvest of unbounded bread,
What would ye more? He

title: A Hymn to the Name and Honour of the Admirable Saint Teresa by Richard Crashaw
content:
LOVE, thou are absolute, sole Lord
Of life and death. To prove the word,
We'll now appeal to none of all
Those thy old soldiers, great and tall,
Ripe

title: Divine Epigrams: To our Lord, upon the Water Made Wine by Richard Crashaw
content:
Thou water turn'st to wine, fair friend of life,
Thy foe, to cross the sweet arts of thy reign,
Distills from thence the tears of wrath and strife,
An

title: But Men Loved Darkness rather than Light by Richard Crashaw
content:
The world's light shines, shine as it will,
The world will love its darkness still.
I doubt though when the world's in hell,
It will not love its dark

title: To the Name above every Name, the Name of Jesus by Richard Crashaw
content:
I sing the Name which None can say
But touch’t with An interiour Ray:
The Name of our New Peace; our Good:
Our Blisse: and Supernaturall Blood:
The Na
